tossups.question: A class of interactions partially named for these structures studied by Dougherty and Gallivan depends on inverse distance to a power less than 2. An interaction named for these structures is described by the controversial Hunter–Sanders model, which explains the effect of EWGs and EDGs on quadrupoles. That interaction named for these structures occurs in “T-shaped” and “parallel displaced” orientations. Electrons in a metal can stabilize a vacant adjacent orbital in an interaction named for these structures that commonly occurs with CO ligands. Instances of these structures have orthogonal orientations in allenes and cumulenes. Parallel instances of these structures mix in conjugated systems. For 10 points, name these structures that arise due to the parallel overlap of p orbitals.

tossups.question: A catalyst system used in one reaction involving these compounds includes molybdenum hexacarbonyl (“muh-LIB-duh-num hexa-KAR-buh-nil”) and resorcinol (“ruh-ZOR-suh-nall”) and is named for Andre Mortreux (“mor-TRUE”). The synthetic estradiol (“ess-truh-DYE-all”) that is found in contraceptives is one of these compounds. In the presence of nickel catalysts, these compounds can trimerize (“TRY-muh-rize”) into arenes (“AIR-eenz”). These compounds can undergo a cross-coupling reaction catalyzed by palladium with aryl (“AIR-ull”) or vinyl halides known as the (*) Sonogashira coupling. This functional group is synthesized from aldehydes (“ALL-duh-hides”) in the Corey-Fuchs reaction. Internal kinds of this functional group can isomerize (“eye-SAHM-uh-rize”) into the terminal kinds of this functional group in the presence of a strong base in their namesake zipper reaction. For 10 points, name this functional group that has a carbon-carbon triple bond.

The synthetic estradiol ("ess-truh-DYE-all") that is found in contraceptives is one of these compounds. For 10 points, name this functional group that has a carbon-carbon triple bond.

tossups.question: Compounds containing this functional group are treated with iodine and either silver benzoate or silver acetate in the Prevost and Woodward reactions, respectively. Cobalt coordinated to four carbonyl ligands is used to convert instances of this functional group into aldehydes. In the presence of a chiral quinine ligand, this functional group reacts with osmium tetroxide to form diols in an asymmetric reaction named for Sharpless. This nucleophilic functional group displaces an acetoxy group after attacking mercury in the first step of an alcohol-forming reaction used to prevent rearrangements. Treating this functional group with borane in THF yields the anti-Markovnikov product. A solution of bromine is often used to detect this functional group. For 10 points, name this functional group in which two carbons are joined by a double bond.
tossups.answer: alkenes [or olefins]
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkenes [or olefins]

packet.name: I – Iowa A, McGill B, NYU A, Toronto A, Warwick A, William _ Mary B

tossups.question: Note to moderator: Read answerline carefully. Ask players to spell their answer if necessary. A class of antitumor agents containing two of these functional groups can form aromatic rings in the Bergman cyclization. A propargylic (“pro-par-JILL-ick”) alcohol is synthesized when this functional group is deprotonated to a strong nucleophile that attacks a carbonyl. Copper and ruthenium complexes are commonly used to catalyze a room-temperature cycloaddition between one of these functional groups and an azide in a click chemistry reaction. This functional group can be selectively reduced to one of two isomers depending on whether the catalyst is sodium in ammonia or a Lindlar catalyst. The simplest compound with this functional group is acetylene, with molecular formula C2H2 (“C-2-H-2”). For 10 points, name this functional group consisting of a triple bond between two carbons.
tossups.answer: alkynes (“al-kines”) [accept triple bonds until read; reject “alkenes” (“al-keens”); reject “alkanes” (“al-kayns”)] (The antitumor agents in the first line are enediynes.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkynes ("al-kines") [accept triple bonds until read; reject "alkenes" ("al-keens"); reject "alkanes" ("al-kayns")] (The antitumor agents in the first line are enediynes.)

tossups.question: The extent of a push-pull mechanism in this type of reaction causes the rotation-inversion controversy. A kinked methyl group allows this reaction to occur through one bond flip and a triplet biradical (“bi-radical”) at a conical intersection. This type of reaction alternates with helix inversions on a crowded rotaxane (“ro-TAX-ane”) to power Ben Feringa’s first unidirectional molecular motor. This reaction turns the square planar compound bistriphenylphosphine platinum chloride yellow. The large change in molecular volume required for this reaction is accommodated by a hula twist. Azobenzene and stilbene act as molecular switches because they undergo this reaction under UV light. When a conjugated aldehyde undergoes this reaction, an opsin is triggered in the retina. This reaction temporarily breaks a stiff pi bond, allowing its substituents to rotate 180 degrees. For 10 points, name this reaction that interconverts alkene isomers.
tossups.answer: cis–trans isomerization [or cis–trans interconversion; or E–Z isomerization or E–Z interconversion; or geometrical isomerization or conversion between geometrical isomers; accept descriptions of switching between cis and trans or E and Z alkenes; prompt on double bond isomerization or alkene isomerization or olefin isomerization; prompt on isomerization or photoisomerization by asking “what is the chemical outcome of the isomerization?”; reject “rearrangement”]

tossups.question: Hydrofluorocarbon refrigerants with these structures added have greatly reduced global warming potential. In a variant of the pinacol [“PIN-uh-call”] coupling, titanium(III) chloride reductively couples carbonyls to form these structures in the McMurry reaction. Complexes of transition metals to these structures are described by the Dewar-Chatt-Duncanson model. Platinum is covalently bonded to three chlorine atoms and complexed to one of these structures in Zeise's salt. Schrock and Grubbs catalysts make two compounds with these structures (*) exchange parts in a namesake metathesis. Since these structures can't rotate, they may display configurational isomerism denoted E-Z or cis-trans. Fatty acids with these structures have a bent shape and are called "unsaturated." For 10 points, name this functional group consisting of a carbon-carbon double bond.
tossups.answer: alkenes [or olefins; before mentioned, accept carbon-carbon double bonds or C-C double bonds and prompt on double bonds or pi bonds; do NOT accept or prompt on "alkanes" or "alkynes"]
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkenes [or olefins; before mentioned, accept carbon-carbon double bonds or C-C double bonds and prompt on double bonds or pi bonds; do NOT accept or prompt on "alkanes" or "alkynes"]

For 10 points, name this functional group consisting of a carbon-carbon double bond.

tossups.question: An amine can be added adjacent to this functional group via the Mannich-like A3 reaction. The ability of silver to activate hydrogens adjacent to this functional group motivated the name of the propargylic (pro-par-GIL-ic) position. Oxygen must be excluded when working with these functional groups in the presence of copper to avoid the Glaser homocoupling, motivating copper-free versions of the Sonogashira coupling. Exposing these functional groups to a superbase triggers almost instantaneous migration in their namesake zipper reaction. Terminal examples of these functional groups have relatively low pKa of about 25, which is attributed to the 50% s-character of their carbon-hydrogen bond. For 10 points, name this functional group with a carbon-carbon triple bond.
tossups.answer: alkynes (al-KAI-nes) [accept the alkyne zipper reaction, terminal alkynes, or internal alkynes; prompt on carbon-carbon triple bonds; do not accept or prompt on "alkenes" (al-KEY-nes) or "alkanes" (al-KAY-nes)]

tossups.question: Schlenk and Brauns name a group of these molecules that exist as biradicals and are paramagnetic with a triplet ground state. The F number is correlated to the log of the retention factor in chromatography of these molecules. Clar’s rule is used to determine the most stable forms of these molecules. A process in which these molecules are broken down uses a system consisting of an alumina matrix, a silica binder, a kaolin filler, and a zeolite catalyst; that process is fluid catalytic cracking. Anthracene and tetracene are examples of the polycyclic aromatic type of these molecules. Bitumen is a mixture of these molecules produced from fractional distillation of petroleum. For 10 points, identify these molecules made up of the elements with atomic numbers 6 and 1.
tossups.answer: hydrocarbons [or polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons; or conjugated hydrocarbons; or Schlenk–Brauns hydrocarbons; prompt on PAHs by asking “what does that stand for?”; anti-prompt on alkanes or alkenes or paraffins or cycloalkanes by asking “can you be less specific?”]

tossups.question: In a March 2019 Nature paper, Michelle Chang’s lab engineered a pathway from S. cattleya to convert lysine into one of these compounds. The synthetic estradiol found in contraceptives, abbreviated E·E, is named for being one of these compounds. The nonstandard amino acids A·H·A and pAzF [pazz-EFF] are chemical handles that react with examples of these compounds like DIFO [DYE-foh]and DBCO [DIB-koh] that have eight-membered rings. In mass spec, these compounds often lose a fragment from the (*) propargyl cation. These compounds are the usual dienophiles in Diels-Alder reactions that form cyclohexadienes. In the presence of copper (I) [“one”], they click onto azides. These compounds can also form carbon-carbon bonds simply by being deprotonated into acetylides. For 10 points, name these compounds formed by dehydrogenating alkenes.
tossups.answer: alkynes [or alkynyl group; do not accept or prompt on “alkanes” or “alkenes”]
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkynes [or alkynyl group; do not accept or prompt on "alkanes" or "alkenes"]

tossups.question: The presence of an electron withdrawing group opposite an electron donating group on one of these compounds lowers its rotational barrier; those highly polarized “push-pull” variants of these compounds have been treated as candidates for molecular logic gates. The Durham precursor is used to form a polymer whose repeating unit contains this functional group. The presence of multiple examples of this functional group defines an ergosterol-binding class of antifungal drugs which includes amphotericin B. The 2000 Nobel in Chemistry was awarded to Heeger, MacDiarmid, and Shirakawa for their work in studying a conductive polymer containing this functional group. Compounds with multiple examples of these functional groups commonly serve as chromophores whose absorbances can be modeled as a particle in a box due to the delocalization of electrons. For 10 points, long conjugated systems often contain many examples of what carbon-carbon double bond containing functional groups?
tossups.answer: alkenes [accept olefins; accept polyenes]
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkenes [accept olefins; accept polyenes]

tossups.question: A catalyst system that acts on these compounds was first developed by Andre Mortreux and is composed of molybdenum hexacarbonyl and phenol additives. A widely used method for synthesis of these molecules involves a base-catalyzed reaction of DAMP at low temperature. These compounds can undergo a palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling reaction with aryl or vinyl halides in a method named for (*) Sonogashira. These molecules can be formed from a reaction that involves lithium–halogen exchange of dibromoalkenes followed by alpha elimination. “Terminal” kinds of these molecules can isomerize into the “internal” kinds of these molecules through an allene intermediate in the presence of a strong base such as sodium amide. Lindlar’s catalyst can hydrogenate these compounds into cis-alkenes. For 10 points, name these compounds that contain a carbon–carbon triple bond.
tossups.answer: alkynes [or terminal alkynes; or internal alkynes; do not accept or prompt on “alkanes” or “alkenes”]

Walter Rep·pe observed that Nickel catalysed the tri·mer·is·a·tion of this functional group to form ben·zene derivatives. tossups.question: Certain thi·o·carb·a·mates form in·i·fert·ers, which control the reactions which form these compounds with products which follow the Schulz-Zimm distribution. The Hildebrand solubility parameters can be used to estimate the interaction parameter chi in the lattice Flory-Huggins model of the solutions of these compounds. Tac·tic·it·y is a measure of the ste·reo·chemistry of these compounds which are formed from alkenes by using a Zie·gler Natta catalyst. tossups.question: The first step of natural taxol synthesis produces a diphosphate and a compound whose name indicates that it is one of these compounds. These compounds are the starting material in a polymerization method driven by the formation of ethylene, which produces large linear polymers from acyclic examples of these compounds. ENB and VND are monomers with this functionality used to produce polymers called EPDM. These compounds are reacted in ADMET, and a four-carbon example polymerizes with styrene to form SBR. A cyclic four-carbon compound of this type can dimerize to form a precursor to a ladderane. These molecules are the simplest that can polymerize through a ring-closing metathesis mechanism. Natural rubber is formed from monomers with this functionality, which are called “cumulated” if their defining functional groups are shared with one atom. Isoprene is an example of, for 10 points, what kind of molecule containing two carbon-carbon double bonds?

tossups.question: Compounds containing two of this functional group in a 1,5-pattern can interconvert in a [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement. In the "conjugate addition" of soft nucleophiles, this functional group is added beta to a carbonyl group. The reaction of phosphonium ylides with ketones or aldehydes favors the production of the Z-stereoisomer of these compounds. Because they rapidly decolorize liquid bromine, bromine can be used to test for their presence. The thermodynamic stability of these compounds increases with substitution; thus, highly substituted ones are favored in eliminations according to Zaitsev's rule. For 10 points, name this functional group that consists of two sp2-carbon atoms linked together by a double bond.
tossups.answer: alkenes [prompt on double bond until read]

tossups.question: An alkylation reactor reacts these compounds with iso·butane and strong acid. The Dewar–Chatt–Duncanson model explains how these compounds act as ligands. Aldehydes are synthesized industrially by carbonylating these compounds. Dis·proportionation during petroleum cracking makes equi·molar hydrogen gas and these compounds as a byproduct. Carbenes add to these compounds to make cyclo·propanes. The simplest Diels–Alder reaction uses one of these compounds as the dieno·phile ("die"-EE-no-"file"). Ziegler–Natta catalysts polymerize these compounds. Markovnikov addition of water to these compounds gives an alcohol. Halogens add to them in the canonical test for un·saturation. tossups.question_sanitized: An alkylation reactor reacts these compounds with isobutane and strong acid. The Dewar-Chatt-Duncanson model explains how these compounds act as ligands. Aldehydes are synthesized industrially by carbonylating these compounds. Disproportionation during petroleum cracking makes equimolar hydrogen gas and these compounds as a byproduct. Carbenes add to these compounds to make cyclopropanes. The simplest Diels-Alder reaction uses one of these compounds as the dienophile ("die"-EE-no-"file"). Ziegler-Natta catalysts polymerize these compounds. Markovnikov addition of water to these compounds gives an alcohol. Halogens add to them in the canonical test for unsaturation. For 10 points, name these hydrocarbons with a carbon-carbon double bond.

tossups.question: These compounds are created in a modification of the pinacol coupling named for McMurry. Potassium peroxymonosulfate and a ketone catalyst react with these compounds to create a dioxirane intermediate in a reaction named for Yian Shi. Treatment of tosylhydrazones with strong bases produces these compounds. Osmium tetroxide catalyzes the dihydroxylation of compounds with this functional group in the Upjohn and Sharpless methods of diol synthesis. In addition to the Shapiro and Bamford-Stevens reactions, methods of producing them include using Grubbs’ catalyst to assist with their namesake metathesis. For 10 points, name these unsaturated hydrocarbons, the simplest of which is ethylene, which have at least one double bond.
tossups.answer: alkenes [or olefins, be careful not to accept alkynes or alkanes, which are different]
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkenes [or olefins, be careful not to accept alkynes or alkanes, which are different]
tossups.question_sanitized: These compounds are created in a modification of the pinacol coupling named for McMurry. Potassium peroxymonosulfate and a ketone catalyst react with these compounds to create a dioxirane intermediate in a reaction named for Yian Shi. Treatment of tosylhydrazones with strong bases produces these compounds. Osmium tetroxide catalyzes the dihydroxylation of compounds with this functional group in the Upjohn and Sharpless methods of diol synthesis. In addition to the Shapiro and Bamford-Stevens reactions, methods of producing them include using Grubbs' catalyst to assist with their namesake metathesis. For 10 points, name these unsaturated hydrocarbons, the simplest of which is ethylene, which have at least one double bond.

tossups.question: One reaction to prepare these compounds adds diazomethylphosphonate esters to aldehydes under basic catalysis and generates vinyl-diazo intermediates. Along with aldehydes, these compounds are the degradation products of alpha, beta-epoxy tosylhydrazones in the Eschenmoser fragmentation. A coupling involving both palladium and copper catalysis, in which these compounds react with aryl halides, is the Sonogashira reaction. These compounds react with azides to form triazole rings in a well-known click-chemistry reaction, and terminal ones can be deprotonated by sodium amide. They are reduced to cis-alkenes by the poisoned Lindlar catalyst. This functional group is composed of two sp-hybridized carbons. For 10 points, name this functional group consisting of a carbon-carbon triple bond.
tossups.answer: alkynes [do not accept "alkenes" or "alkanes"; if necessary, ask teams to spell their answer]
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkynes [do not accept "alkenes" or "alkanes"; if necessary, ask teams to spell their answer]
tossups.question_sanitized: One reaction to prepare these compounds adds diazomethylphosphonate esters to aldehydes under basic catalysis and generates vinyl-diazo intermediates. Along with aldehydes, these compounds are the degradation products of alpha, beta-epoxy tosylhydrazones in the Eschenmoser fragmentation. A coupling involving both palladium and copper catalysis, in which these compounds react with aryl halides, is the Sonogashira reaction. These compounds react with azides to form triazole rings in a well-known click-chemistry reaction, and terminal ones can be deprotonated by sodium amide. They are reduced to cis-alkenes by the poisoned Lindlar catalyst. This functional group is composed of two sp-hybridized carbons. For 10 points, name this functional group consisting of a carbon-carbon triple bond.

tossups.question: Symmetric examples of these compounds are formed under anhydrous conditions in a coupling reaction using sodium metal. Methods of preparing these compounds include reacting thioacetals with hydrogen gas and Raney Nickel, and reacting ketones with hydrazine under basic conditions. In IR spec, due to stretching and scissoring of bonds, these compounds have peaks in the 3000 to 2850 inverse centimeter and 1470 to 1450 inverse centimeter range. These compounds are produced when reacting Grignard reagents with water. Conformational isomers of these molecules, such as gauche and eclipsed, can be visualized with Newman projections. These compounds have general formula C sub n H sub two n plus two. For 10 points, name these hydrocarbons that only have single bonds, as exemplified by methane.
tossups.answer: alkanes [or paraffins; prompt on saturated hydrocarbons until it is read; do not accept "alkenes" or "alkynes"]
tossups.answer_sanitized: alkanes [or paraffins; prompt on saturated hydrocarbons until it is read; do not accept "alkenes" or "alkynes"]
tossups.question_sanitized: Symmetric examples of these compounds are formed under anhydrous conditions in a coupling reaction using sodium metal. These compounds are produced when reacting Grignard reagents with water. Conformational isomers of these molecules, such as gauche and eclipsed, can be visualized with Newman projections. These compounds have general formula C sub n H sub two n plus two. For 10 points, name these hydrocarbons that only have single bonds, as exemplified by methane.

bonuses.leadin: Copper catalysts are typically used to couple molecules with this property in the Ullmann reaction. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Copper catalysts are typically used to couple molecules with this property in the Ullmann reaction. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this property possessed by planar cyclic molecules with 4n+2 pi electrons. Examples of these molecules include benzene and pyrene.

In the Sonogashira reaction, two cycles catalyzed by copper and palladium perform the cross-coupling of aryl or vinyl halides into these functional groups. Lindlar catalysts can be used to hydrogenate these functional groups.

Unlike Grignard reagents that would attack the carbonyl group, these organocuprates can perform conjugate additions onto unsaturated ketones and esters. These reagents are alkylated copper ions ionically bonded to lithium.
parts_sanitized: Name this property possessed by planar cyclic molecules with 4n+2 pi electrons. Examples of these molecules include benzene and pyrene.

Unlike Grignard reagents that would attack the carbonyl group, these organocuprates can perform conjugate additions onto unsaturated ketones and esters. These reagents are alkylated copper ions ionically bonded to lithium.
answers: Gilman reagents [accept lower order cuprates; prompt on lithium dialkyl cuprates]

bonuses.leadin: This compound was first synthesized by James Bruce and Richard Willstätter in 1907. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This compound was first synthesized by James Bruce and Richard Willstatter in 1907. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this compound whose hydroxylated derivative can be synthesized in the Norrish-Yang reaction. Due to strain, this compound’s “puckered” conformation possesses bond angles of slightly less than 90 degrees.
Cyclobutane is a cyclic one of these organic compounds whose carbon backbone consists of carbon-carbon single bonds.
These compounds can be produced by fusing together multiple cyclobutane molecules. The membranes of anaerobic ammonium-oxidating bacteria mainly consist of lipids linked to these compounds.
parts_sanitized: Name this compound whose hydroxylated derivative can be synthesized in the Norrish-Yang reaction. Due to strain, this compound's "puckered" conformation possesses bond angles of slightly less than 90 degrees.

answers_sanitized: alkanes ("al-KAINZ") [accept cycloalkanes or cyclic alkanes; reject "alkenes;" reject "alkynes"]

bonuses.leadin: This rule gives the opposite result of Hofmann’s rule, which is valid for sterically hindered molecules, in contrast to this rule. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this rule that determines the major product in an elimination reaction based on the degree of substitution.

Zaitsev’s rule is explained by the relative stability of alkenes, with more-substituted alkenes being more stable because electron density is donated from these bonds. They are formed from the overlap of s orbitals.
A good leaving group, such as this aromatic sulfonyl-containing group, is a weak base, allowing it to leave the substrate with a lone pair of electrons in elimination reactions.
answers: Zaitsev’s rule [accept Zaitsev alkenes or Zaitsev products; accept Saytzeff or Saytzev in place of “Zaitsev”]

packet.name: B – McMaster, UBC A, UVA A, UW A, UW B

tosyl group [or toluenesulfonyl group; or Ts or Tos; accept tosylates]

bonuses.leadin: The discovery of this molecule led to rapid growth in organometallic chemistry and a Nobel Prize for Fischer and Wilkinson. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this sandwich compound in which two cyclopentadienyl ligands are coordinated to the central iron atom.

This model, which is named for three chemists, helped explain the structure and remarkable stability of ferrocene. This model describes donation of electron density from ligands to transition metal d-orbitals in a form of pi backbonding.

The Dewar–Chatt–Duncanson is applicable to coordination compounds with these molecules as ligands due to their pi bonds. These molecules contain at least two sp2-hybridized carbon atoms due to the presence of a double bond.

packet.name: J – Berkeley B, Bristol A, Columbia B, Florida B, Imperial A, Mississippi State, Northwestern, Texas A&M A

bonuses.leadin: The addition of hydrogen bromide to one of these molecules obeys Markovnikov’s rule. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these unsaturated hydrocarbons with at least one carbon–carbon double bond.

In the presence of this type of compound, the addition of hydrogen bromide to alkenes is anti-Markovnikov. The efficiency of the decomposition of this type of compound is limited by the cage effect, which slows diffusive separation.

This two-step reaction converts alkenes to anti-Markovnikov alcohols, unlike oxymercuration-demercuration. Alkyl boranes with steric (“STAIR-ick”) hindrance enhance the regioselectivity (“REE-jee-oh-selectivity”) of this reaction.
answers: radical initiator [prompt on peroxides or hydrogen peroxide or H2O2 by asking “what is the role of the peroxide in the reaction?”]

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about homologation reactions, for 10 points each.
parts: Homologation reactions generally extend a carbon chain by adding one of these groups, which share their name with the simplest carbene (“car-bean”). When these functional groups are between two carbon atoms, they are called “bridges.”

Aryl ketones are converted to this functional group through the addition of potassium tert-butoxide (“turt-bʼyoot-oxide”) in the Seyferth homologation. Lindlar’s catalyst partially hydrogenates this functional group’s triple bond.
This homologation reaction can produce beta-amino acids through a crucial intermediary step in which a diazoketone is rearranged to form a ketene, typically with a silver (I) (”one”) oxide catalyst.

packet.name: N_ Kentucky, Virginia, Ohio State, Columbia B, Brandeis, Amherst, Carleton, Florida B

answers_sanitized: methylene group [or methylene bridges; prompt on CH2]

bonuses.leadin: As ligands, these compounds both donate sigma electrons to a metal and accept pi electrons, which causes their complexes to resemble cyclo·propane rings. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these organic compounds. As ligands, their covalent compounds are described by the Dewar–Chatt–Duncanson model and include Zeise’s (“ZAY-suh’s”) salt.

Pi backbonding leads to a small but measurable increase in this quantity for alkenes bound to metals, compared to free alkenes. For carbon-carbon bonds, this quantity is usually about one-and-a-half angstroms.

Since alkenes form part-covalent, part-dative bonds with metals, they are in both of these two major classes of electron-donating ligands. Name both classes, each identified by a letter of the alphabet.
answers_sanitized: L-type ligands AND X-type ligands [accept either order]

bonuses.leadin: The metal-catalyzed decomposition of diazocarbonyl compounds can be used to form this functional group. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this functional group which is exemplified by a neutral carbon species with six valence electrons. This functional group is coordinated to ruthenium in the Grubbs catalyst.
The Grubbs catalyst serves to catalyze the metathesis of these compounds. That reaction involves the exchange of substituents on the characteristic carbon-carbon double bond of these compounds.
The reaction of a carbene with an olefin yields derivatives of this compound. Bent bonds describe why this compound is stable despite having high ring strain due to three 60 degree bond angles.

bonuses.leadin: Kenichi Fukui developed the Frontier Molecular Orbital Theory to explain these rules. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this set of rules that can be illustrated with orbital correlation diagrams. Reactions governed by this set of rules are given a unique descriptor enclosed in brackets.

The Woodward–Hoffman rules are used to explain the stereochemistry of pericyclic reactions, such as this reaction that forms cyclohexene derivatives. This reaction combines a conjugated diene and a dienophile (“die-EE-no-file”).
The dienophile in the Diels–Alder reaction is often a compound with one of these functional groups, which contain a carbon-carbon double bond.
answers: Woodward–Hoffman rules [or pericyclic selection rules; do not accept or prompt on “selection rules”]
alkenes [or olefins; do not accept or prompt on “alkanes” or “alkynes”]

bonuses.leadin: Welcome to the chemistry deli! Answer the following about metallocene sandwich compounds, for 10 points each.
parts: Diaconescu and Cummins synthesized an “inverted” form of this sandwich compound first synthesized by Mueller-Westerhoff and Streitwieser. Two cyclo·octate·traene dianions bind to the central element in this compound that, similar to nickelocene, is a green, volatile solid.

Covalent bonding in uranocene occurs between the ligand pi orbitals and these two sets of atomic orbitals of uranium. These two sets of atomic orbitals have angular momentum quantum number 2 and 3. Name both.
Uranocene is mostly of academic interest, but zirconocene/MAO systems catalyze the polymerization of these functional groups. Ziegler–Natta catalysts are used to polymerize these functional groups.

bonuses.leadin: This reaction begins with the deprotonation of a phthalamide and produces a phthalhydrazide by-product. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this organic reaction which traditionally uses potassium phthalamide followed by hydrazine to produce primary amines. The development of alternative reagents which hydrolyse more readily also allows the production of secondary amines.

The Gabriel synthesis converts this class of compounds to primary amines. These compounds can be synthesised by reacting an alkene with [emphasise] a specific group of elements in a ‘decolourising’ addition reaction.
Amines contain hydrogen atoms or other substituent groups bonded to a central atom of this element. In ammonia, three hydrogen atoms are bonded to an atom of this element.

bonuses.leadin: These organic compounds can be synthesised into polymers by Ziegler-Natta [ZEE-gluh-NATT-uh] catalysts. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this homologous series of unsaturated hydrocarbons which contain at least one carbon-carbon double bond. They can be expressed using the general formula C·n·H·2·n.

The simplest alkene, ethene, can be used to produce ethanol through a hydration reaction. That reaction is most commonly catalysed by this triprotic acid, adsorbed onto a porous support such as silica gel.

Ethene can also be oxidised to form acetaldehyde through this cyclic process, in the presence of a palladium (II) chloride catalyst. In a subsequent step of this process, the palladium chloride catalyst is regenerated using copper (II) chloride as an oxidising agent.
answers: alkenes [do NOT accept or prompt on alkanes or alkynes: ask competitors to repeat and/or spell their answers for clarification if necessary]

bonuses.leadin: Organic chemistry isn’t just about carbon, hydrogen, oxygen and nitrogen! For 10 points each:
parts: Compounds of this metal are called organostannanes. They are coupled to aryl halides in the Stille reaction.

The Julia reaction reacts phenyl sulfones with carbonyl compounds and reduces the product with sodium amalgam to form these functional groups. These functional groups are also known as olefins, and they can be classified as E or Z.

Z-alkenes are the favoured product of this modification of the Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons reaction. In this reaction, a stabilized phosphonate anion reacts with a carbonyl in the presence of KHMDS and 18-crown-6.
One early Seuss book dealing with the further adventures of Bartholomew Cubbins gave this non-Newtonian fluid, a suspension of cornstarch in water, its common name.
This first children’s book by Seuss was rejected by over twenty publishing companies before its publication, and describes Marco’s imagined encounters during his walk home along the titular road.

The story ‘The Sneetches’, describing the tensions between the Star-Bellied and Plain Sneetches and how they were defused by Theodore McMonkey McBean, was intended by Seuss as a satirical response to this particular racial and religious intolerance.
answers: Still-Gennari Reaction 12 And now all good quizzers will surely rejoice/At these lesser-known writings of Doctor Seuss. For 10 points each:

Anti-semitism [accept anything about a negative response to Jews]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Organic chemistry isn't just about carbon, hydrogen, oxygen and nitrogen! For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Compounds of this metal are called organostannanes. They are coupled to aryl halides in the Stille reaction.

The Julia reaction reacts phenyl sulfones with carbonyl compounds and reduces the product with sodium amalgam to form these functional groups. These functional groups are also known as olefins, and they can be classified as E or Z.

Z-alkenes are the favoured product of this modification of the Horner-Wadsworth-Emmons reaction. In this reaction, a stabilized phosphonate anion reacts with a carbonyl in the presence of KHMDS and 18-crown-6.
One early Seuss book dealing with the further adventures of Bartholomew Cubbins gave this non-Newtonian fluid, a suspension of cornstarch in water, its common name.
This first children's book by Seuss was rejected by over twenty publishing companies before its publication, and describes Marco's imagined encounters during his walk home along the titular road.

The story 'The Sneetches', describing the tensions between the Star-Bellied and Plain Sneetches and how they were defused by Theodore McMonkey McBean, was intended by Seuss as a satirical response to this particular racial and religious intolerance.

bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions about Grubbs’ catalyst, for 10 points each:
parts: Grubbs’ catalyst is commonly used to perform metathesis on these compounds which contain a carbon-carbon double bond.

The initiation rate of the Grubbs catalyst can be increased by using derivatives of this compound as ligand. This compound can be considered as a benzene with one C-H unit replaced by a nitrogen atom.

Grubb’s catalyst can be used to form these interlocked structures via a “clipping” mechanism. These structures consist of a macrocycle component wrapped around an acyclic axle and are used in molecular motors.

This reaction sees the reaction between a diene and a dienophile such as a substituted alkene to form a cyclohexene system.
This rule states that for bridged rings, a double bond cannot be placed at the bridgehead owing to a combination of ring and angle strain.

bonuses.leadin: The simplest one of these compounds stimulates the development of flowers and ripening of fruit in its role as a plant hormone. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this group of organic compounds with a double bond, exemplified by ethylene.
A double bond consists of a sigma bond and one of these bonds formed from overlapping p orbitals.
In molecular orbital theory, this type of orbital is located outside of the nuclei, and is denoted with an asterisk. They are higher in energy than another type of molecular orbital.

bonuses.leadin: Bonds between carbon and this element can be generated in the Buchwald-Hartwig coupling. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this element which is bonded to at least one alkyl group in an amine.
Amines can also be generated by reduction of this functional group. This functional group can be formed from dehydration of amides and is hydrolyzed by acid into a carboxylic acid.
In the reductive amination of ketones, primary amines form imines as an intermediate, but secondary amines form these compounds. These compounds undergo C-alkylation in the Stork reactions.
answers_sanitized: nitriles [prompt on cyanide; prompt on cyano]
enamines (pr. EEN-AMINES) [prompt on alkenes; prompt on beta-unsaturated amines]

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name some achievements of the namesake of the Woodwardian era.
parts: The Woodward-Hoffmann rules are used in this branch of “three-dimensional” chemistry, which studies the arrangement of atoms within molecules. Enantiomers are this type of isomer.
To everyone’s amazement, Woodward discovered the total synthesis of this cobalt-containing vitamin with Eschenmoser in 1973, after Dorothy Hodgkin had already determined its crystal structure.
In a reaction named for Woodward, vicinal diols are produced from these functional groups after they’re reacted with iodine and an acetate in the presence of water.
answers: alkenes [accept olefins; do not accept nor prompt on “alkynes,” “alkanes” or “paraffins”]
answers_sanitized: stereochemistry [accept stereoisomer, stereoisomerism and other word forms]
alkenes [accept olefins; do not accept nor prompt on "alkynes," "alkanes" or "paraffins"]

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name some terms somehow having to do with nitriles.
parts: Nitriles consist of carbons triple bonded to nitrogens, similar to this functional group which consists of carbon-carbon triple bonds.

Nitriles can be produced by the Schmidt reaction which is catalyzed by this type of catalyst. The aldol condensation can be performed with one of these catalysts, which along with a zinc catalyst is required for the Clemmensen reduction.
This synthesis reaction gives particularly good yields, by reacting primary aliphatic halides and alkali metal cyanides.
answers: alkynes [do not accept nor prompt on “alkanes,” “alkenes,” “paraffins” or “olefins”]
answers_sanitized: alkynes [do not accept nor prompt on "alkanes," "alkenes," "paraffins" or "olefins"]

parts_sanitized: Nitriles consist of carbons triple bonded to nitrogens, similar to this functional group which consists of carbon-carbon triple bonds.

Nitriles can be produced by the Schmidt reaction which is catalyzed by this type of catalyst. The aldol condensation can be performed with one of these catalysts, which along with a zinc catalyst is required for the Clemmensen reduction.
This synthesis reaction gives particularly good yields, by reacting primary aliphatic halides and alkali metal cyanides.

bonuses.leadin: This functional group is created by the E2 reaction mechanism, which uses a strong base to remove a proton and force the creation of a double bond. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this functional group, consisting of a carbon chain with at least one carbon-carbon double bond.

In this two-step Markovnikov reaction, a certain acetate reacts with an alkene to form a three membered ring, which is then opened by water. After this reaction, sodium borohydride is applied to remove a namesake substance and yield an alcohol.

In this reaction, catalysts like Grubbs’ catalyst can be used to combine with two alkenes to form a metallacyclobutane, which then breaks up, leaving the carbons from one alkene double-bonded to the carbons from the other alkene.
answers_sanitized: alkenes [do not accept alkanes or alkynes]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This functional group is created by the E2 reaction mechanism, which uses a strong base to remove a proton and force the creation of a double bond. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this functional group, consisting of a carbon chain with at least one carbon-carbon double bond.

In this reaction, catalysts like Grubbs' catalyst can be used to combine with two alkenes to form a metallacyclobutane, which then breaks up, leaving the carbons from one alkene double-bonded to the carbons from the other alkene.
